raw_consumer_statement:
Your rewritten explanation is already clear and well-structured, but heres a slightly refined version for added clarity and polish : I initially set up an auto payment of {$120.00} per week for my Citibank credit card to be withdrawn from my debit card. However, on XX/XX/XXXX, I discovered that Citibank had started charging me {$250.00} every two weeks instead, beginning in XXXX. 

When I contacted customer service, a representative claimed that I had made this change via the mobile app on XX/XX/XXXX and that a confirmation was sent. However, I never received any such confirmation. Additionally, a previous representative informed me that there was no record of any confirmation being sent. 

I did not make any changes to my auto-pay settings, so Citibank must have made the alteration on their end. I have no evidence on my end that these settings were ever updated. The only related email I received was an unusual message stating : Your AutoPay preferences for your XXXX XXXX XXXX have been updated online, with a payment of {$0.00} due each billing cycle to your Checking account from Citibank. 

This situation is both confusing and troubling, as I did not authorize or initiate any changes to my account settings. 

This version emphasizes the timeline and inconsistencies more clearly while maintaining a professional tone.
